blurb:
His name is Goober and he's a chocolate lab, which honestly tells you most of what you need to know. He's probably friendly, food-motivated, and thinks every person he meets is his new best friend. Labs are like that. His bio is pretty sparse too, so we're going with what we can see in his photo and what we know about chocolate labs in general.

He looks like the kind of dog who greets you at the door like you've been gone for years when you just went to get the mail. Labs are notorious for being happy, loyal, and slightly obsessed with tennis balls, treats, and swimming. If you have a pool, lake access, or even just a kiddie pool in the backyard, he's probably going to lose his mind with excitement.

Chocolate labs tend to be the goofballs of the lab family, and with a name like Goober, he's probably living up to that reputation. He's likely great with kids, other dogs, and anyone willing to pet him or throw something for him to fetch. These dogs are made for families who want a steady, loving companion who's always up for whatever you're doing.

Our Pals Place is in Marietta and you can contact them through their Petfinder listing to learn more about Goober and get the adoption process started.

blurb:
Someone named this cat Pork Tenderloin and honestly, that's the kind of energy we need more of in the world. He's an adult male with short hair and what appears to be a very serious expression in his photo, like he's contemplating whether his name is actually helping his adoption chances.

This listing doesn't have much of a bio, so we're going off his photo and that incredible name. He looks like the kind of cat who would sit in your kitchen while you cook dinner, judging your seasoning choices but staying close enough to supervise. Definitely has opinions about things. Probably the type to claim one specific chair in your house and make it clear that's his spot now.

Good Mews doesn't post detailed bios for all their cats, but they're a no-kill, cage-free shelter where you can visit and get to know the animals before deciding. Pork Tenderloin is waiting there to meet someone who appreciates both a good name and whatever personality comes with it. Submit an inquiry online or call to set up a visit.

blurb:
Hazel Jordan is a Nova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever, which is basically a dog breed most people have never heard of but should probably know about. She doesn't have a proper bio either, so we're going off her photo and breed knowledge. Tollers are the redheads of the retriever world, and they're known for being energetic, smart, and having a slightly mischievous streak.

She's got that beautiful red coat and probably the personality to match. These dogs were bred to lure ducks by playing on the shoreline, so she's likely playful, athletic, and probably thinks fetch is the greatest invention ever. If you throw something, she's bringing it back. If you don't throw something, she's probably going to find something anyway and drop it at your feet with a look that says "your move."

She's going to need someone who appreciates an active, intelligent dog. Tollers do great with families and usually love water if you happen to live near a lake or creek. She's probably going to want to be involved in whatever you're doing, whether that's yard work, hiking, or just sitting on the couch.

Our Pals Place handles adoptions through their Petfinder page. Contact them directly to learn more about Hazel Jordan and start the adoption process.

blurb:
Patriot is a senior male cat who's probably seen some things in his time. The shelter doesn't have a detailed bio for him, but his photo shows a handsome orange tabby with white markings who looks like he knows how to settle into a comfortable routine. Senior cats often make the best companions once you get past the idea that older means less fun.

He's at that age where he's done with drama and just wants a calm place to spend his days. Senior cats usually know what they like and don't waste time with nonsense. They're also grateful for a second chance in a way that's different from younger animals. Patriot probably won't be climbing your curtains or knocking things off your counters at 3am.

If you've been thinking about adding a cat to your household, seniors like Patriot are often overlooked but make incredible pets. They're past the destructive kitten phase and ready to just be good company. Visit Good Mews to meet him and see if you click.

blurb:

Sometimes the cats without detailed bios are the ones who surprise you most when you meet them in person.

She's at Good Mews, which is a cage-free environment, so she's been socializing with other cats and getting used to people coming and going. That usually means she's adaptable and not easily stressed by changes. Adult female cats like Beignet tend to be independent but affectionate when they want to be.

Without a proper bio to go on, meeting Beignet in person is really the only way to know if she's the right fit. Good Mews lets you spend time with their cats before deciding, which is exactly what you'd want to do with someone like her who's a bit of a mystery right now.

blurb:
Luke is a young German Shepherd and Belgian Malinois mix who doesn't have much of a bio on file yet, so we're making one up based on his photo. Look at that face and tell us he doesn't have opinions about everything. He's probably the kind of dog who watches you make coffee in the morning and has thoughts about your technique.

He's got that shepherd intensity written all over him, which means he's going to be smart, loyal, and probably need a job to do. Even if that job is just following you around the house making sure you're doing everything correctly. These breeds are known for being devoted to their people and having energy to burn.

Since he's young and clearly a working breed mix, he's going to need someone who can keep up with him mentally and physically. A fenced yard would be perfect, and if you're the kind of person who likes long hikes or runs, even better. He's the type who will probably excel at whatever training you throw at him once he settles in.
